sections.paragraph:
εὐαγγελίζω [εὖ, ἀγγελίζω ‘announce’; ‘bring/announce good news’, such as a military victory]

—1. ‘pass on information that spells good tidings to the recipient’, bring/announce good news Lk 1:19; 2:10; 1 Th 3:6; Rv 10:7; 14:6. A transition is readily made to

—2. ‘spread good tidings of God’s beneficial concern’

—a. publish good news/tidings, of proclamation by John the Baptist Lk 3:18; by Jesus 4:18, 43; 7:22; 8:1; 9:6; 20:1; by unspecified parties 16:16.

—b. specifically of proclamation w. focus on God’s saving action, explicit or implicit, in connection w. Jesus: publish the good news, publish the gospel Ac 8:4 (εὐ. τόν λόγον), 12, 35; 10:36; 11:20; 13:32; Ro 1:15; 2 Cor 11:7; Gal 1:8; Eph 3:8; 1 Pt 1:12; abs. Lk 9:6; Ac 14:7; Ro 15:20; 1 Cor 1:17; 9:16, 18.
